evals.prompt: I have code another agent generated in this repo and a markdown rubric at .generated-code-eval/loop-eval.md. Evaluate the code against that spec, score every expectation from 0 to 100 with severity, respect the ignore rules, then generate and open an HTML report.
evals.expected_output: A results JSON file and an HTML report with per-expectation scoring, weighted overall grade, and evidence-backed findings.

evals.prompt: Please audit the generated TypeScript changes in this project against my spec file review/generated-agent-audit.md. The spec has expected file structure, exclusions for formatting noise, and a few exceptions. I need a browser-opened HTML scorecard, not just prose.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads the user rubric, inspects the relevant code, writes structured findings JSON, and renders an HTML report that opens locally.

evals.prompt: I want a rubric-based QA pass on code produced by a different agent. The markdown file docs/generated-code-rubric.md already lists expectations, code references, and ignore conditions. Run the evaluation from the project root and give me a weighted final grade in an HTML report.
evals.expected_output: A root-scoped generated-code evaluation that honors rubric metadata, produces one finding per expectation, computes a weighted overall grade, and writes an HTML report.
